Slab only: just the door itself, no jamb. Used when the existing jamb is sound and only the door needs replacement. Requires cutting hinge mortises and drilling knob/deadbolt holes on-site (or ordering pre-machined).

Prefer prehung when practical. Field-fitting a slab to an existing frame is difficult and rarely produces a properly-sealed door.

Handing (Left-Hand vs. Right-Hand, Inswing vs. Outswing)

Stand outside the door looking in.

  • Right-hand: hinges on your right.
  • Left-hand: hinges on your left.
  • Inswing: door swings toward you (into the house).
  • Outswing: door swings away from you (outward).

Full handing callout: LHI (Left-Hand Inswing), RHI (Right-Hand Inswing), LHO, RHO. Most residential entry doors are inswing.

Common mistake: ordering a right-hand door for a left-hand opening. It doesn't fit — it's not just cosmetic. Always confirm handing when standing at the actual doorway.

Patio & Sliding Doors

Two common styles:

  • Sliding glass patio door — two or three panels, one or more move on a track. Standard sizes: 5′0″, 6′0″, 8′0″, 9′0″ wide × 80″ tall.
  • French / hinged patio door — two hinged panels meeting in the middle. Standard: 5′0″ and 6′0″ wide.

Handing on sliders: which panel is fixed vs. which slides. From outside looking in: OX (fixed left, slider right), XO (slider left, fixed right), OXO (three-panel with slider in middle).

Storm Doors

  • Standard slab sizes match entry doors: 32″, 34″, 36″ wide × 80″ tall.
  • Handing: match the swing of the entry door underneath (usually).
  • Verify existing jamb depth is compatible — standard storm door frames fit 3-1/4″ jambs; deeper jambs may need spacers.

Fire-Rated Doors (Garage-to-House)

The door between an attached garage and the living space must be fire-rated per RCO R302.5.1: 20-minute minimum, self-closing hinges, tight fit. Metal-clad or solid wood 1-3/8″ minimum thickness. Applies to any garage-to-house door regardless of original installation date.

Gutters

Choose your source of measurements.

Gutters — Hand Measurements

Gutters follow the eave line. Ground measurement with a long tape works fine for most residential jobs.

Total Eave Linear Feet

Walk each eave and record LF. Total for the whole house. Gutters typically run in continuous sections between corners and end caps.

  • Standard sizes: 5″ K-style (residential) or 6″ K-style (heavier residential / lower-slope commercial). Confirm what's on the house now.
  • Half-round or box gutter: less common; note if present.

Downspouts — Count & Placement

Rule of thumb: one downspout per 30′ of gutter run for standard residential rainfall. More on steep roofs, heavy tree cover, or bigger square footage draining to a single run.

  • Walk and count existing downspouts.
  • Note where they discharge: grade, splash block, buried leader, ground gutter.
  • Note diameter: 2″ × 3″ (standard) or 3″ × 4″ (heavy flow).

Corners, Elbows, End Caps

  • Inside miters: where two gutter runs meet at an inside corner. Count.
  • Outside miters: outside corners. Count.
  • End caps: where a gutter run terminates without a corner. Count.
  • Elbows: two per downspout is standard (A elbow at gutter to route out, B elbow at the wall). Add offset elbows for tricky routing.

Gutter Guards / Leaf Protection

Sold by the LF. Equals total gutter LF unless partial coverage.

  • Screen / mesh: low-cost, DIY-friendly.
  • Solid cover (K-guard, LeafFilter, etc.): higher performance, higher cost.
  • Micro-mesh: premium tier, near-total leaf exclusion.

Slope / Fall

Not a takeoff number but important: gutters need 1/16″ to 1/4″ per 10′ of fall toward each downspout. On existing gutters, verify with a hose or a level — sagging gutters (standing water) are a code and warranty problem worth calling out in the scope.

Gutters — Reading Reports

Aerial roof reports give you the eave LF you need. Downspouts and corners still require a ground walk.

What Roof Reports Give You

Any roof report (Hover, Roofr, EagleView) provides total eave linear feet. That's the gutter LF number — use it directly.

Trust: total eave LF from a report is accurate within roughly 1%.

What You Still Need to Walk

  • Downspout count and placement (reports don't identify existing downspouts).
  • Downspout discharge routing — grade, splash blocks, buried leaders. Impacts scope pricing.
  • Existing gutter condition — is this a re-line or full replace?
  • Corner count (inside vs. outside miters).

Fascia

Choose your source of measurements.

Fascia — Hand Measurements

Fascia runs along the eaves and rakes. Match existing profile unless replacing everything.

Total Fascia Linear Feet

Fascia LF equals total eave LF plus total rake LF (for houses with sub-fascia at the rakes). Some houses have fascia only at the eaves and gable-end trim at rakes — walk it.

Width / Profile

Standard fascia widths on residential:

  • 1x6 (5-1/2″ face) — older / smaller homes.
  • 1x8 (7-1/4″ face) — most common on modern residential.
  • 2x8 or 2x10 — heavier profile, some modern styles, commercial.

Measure the face of the existing fascia to match. If replacing wood with aluminum wrap or PVC, order the wrap sized for the existing width.

Material Options

  • Wood (pine, cedar) with paint: traditional, requires periodic repainting.
  • Aluminum coil wrap over existing wood: low-maintenance overlay. LF of coil.
  • Solid aluminum fascia: replacement fascia in aluminum. LF.
  • PVC / cellular composite: premium, paintable, rot-proof. LF.

Corners & Returns

Where the eave fascia meets the rake fascia at a gable end, there's a mitered return. Count these transitions — each is a labor factor and a small material factor for the miter.

Fascia — Reading Reports

Aerial reports give you fascia LF but not width or profile.

What Reports Give You

  • Eave LF — direct fascia number for eaves.
  • Rake LF — use if the house has fascia at the rakes; not all do.
Still need to walk: fascia width, material, condition (wrap-over vs. full replace), corner return count.

Soffit

Choose your source of measurements.

Soffit — Hand Measurements

Soffit is the horizontal underside of the eave overhang. Width is what surprises people.

Soffit Width

Measure from the exterior wall out to the back of the fascia. This is the width of the soffit panel run.

  • 12″ — typical minimal overhang.
  • 16″–24″ — standard residential.
  • 36″ or more — deep-overhang modern or ranch styles.

The wider the overhang, the more soffit material per LF of eave.

Linear Feet & Area

Soffit runs the total eave LF (same as gutters and fascia). Total soffit area = eave LF × soffit width.

Example: 180 LF of eaves with a 24″ (2′) soffit = 360 sqft of soffit.

Order in LF for aluminum coil (which is sold by LF of coil, then covers a given width). Order in square feet for vinyl or aluminum panel soffit which is sold by the square foot or 20′ panel unit.

Vented vs. Solid

Soffit is where attic intake ventilation happens under a balanced ridge-and-soffit system. Vented soffit panels have perforations; solid panels don't.

  • Most retrofits use vented panels along the full eave for maximum intake (unless there's a specific reason to limit).
  • If the existing soffit is solid without gable vents or ridge vent, the attic likely doesn't meet current R806 ventilation requirements — note this as a code-driven scope adjustment.

Corner Geometry

At outside corners of the house, soffit runs meet in one of two ways:

  • Mitered corner: soffit meets at a 45° miter. More labor, cleaner look.
  • Boxed corner (also called hip return): soffit runs straight to the corner and returns square. Faster labor.

Count corners so labor is priced correctly.

Soffit — Reading Reports

Some Hover reports include soffit area directly. Aerial reports do not.

Hover for Soffit

Hover's siding-inclusive reports can include soffit area when the photo capture includes upward-looking shots that show the eave underside. Coverage varies by report tier.

Verify: the report's stated soffit width against what you measure. Reports may default to a standard overhang assumption if the width isn't clearly captured.

Calculating from an Aerial Report

Aerial reports give eave LF but not soffit width. Combine:

  • Get eave LF from the roof report.
  • Walk the house and measure soffit width at a few representative points.
  • Multiply. Add corners.

⚡ Quick Inspection

First, pin the property. Drag the pin or type the address, then choose the client.

⚡ Quick Inspection

Who is this inspection for?

📁 Company Documents

Official Cardinal forms and paperwork, organized by category. View or download on any device.
These are blank master copies for print. For a client contract, open the client's profile and use the approval workflow — it prefills their info, pulls the job price, and routes for signature.

🛡️ Insurance documents are coming soon.
Claims paperwork, carrier forms, and supplement templates will live here.

👤 My Profile

Your name and contact info appear as “Prepared By / Inspector” on every estimate and inspection report you create.

🔔 Phone Notifications

Get lock-screen alerts on this device when contracts are signed, jobs are approved, and jobs complete.

iPhone first step: in Safari tap Share ↑ → Add to Home Screen, then open Cardinal from that icon and come back here.

👥 Client Directory

⚡ Activity Feed

Everything happening across the team, newest first.

💬 Communications

Notes, call logs, and document activity for this client. Tag a teammate with @name.

🕵 Audit Log

Sign-ins across the team and how long each session stayed active. Sessions ping every minute while the app is open; “online” means activity in the last 2 minutes.

📅 Schedule Board

Jobs 🔨, material drops 🚚, and appointments 📅 for the next 14 days. Tap a day to add or edit.

Add project

📋 Roofing Inspection Checklist

👤 Client Information
📋 System Baseline Specifications (Mandatory Data)
🔦 Mandatory Attic Inspection

The Rule: You cannot accurately diagnose a roof from the outside alone. Request attic access on every inspection to protect the company from deck failure or hidden mold liability.

💥 Surface & Component Defects
🏭 Roof Extrusions
📊 Overall Roof Condition
📝 Notes

🔍 General Inspection Checklist

The Law of the Lead: An invitation to a home is a legal invitation to audit the entire asset. Evaluate every property to ensure the exterior envelope is fully sealed.
👤 Client Information
🔍 Phase 1: General Exterior Asset Assessment

Rate each major asset class.

📝 Notes

🚩 Leads from this inspection

These assets were rated Poor or Critical — turn them into estimates while you’re on site.

🚩 New Lead

Primary Contact * Required
Location Information * Required
Job Details
🛡️ Insurance Details
Assign Lead
📅 Initial Appointment
Notes / Tools

+ Add appointment

✍️ Client signature

Have the client sign with a finger or stylus. The signature and today’s date are placed on the acceptance line.
